535.title: Repeat the last note, including non-printable characters

536.title: Repeat Last Note
536.description:
In character entry, it repeats your last input.
In chorded entry, it is used for words with repeating letters.

533.title: Repeat Last Note
533.description:
In character entry, it repeats your last input.
In chorded entry, it is used for words with repeating letters.

540.name: MIRROR_R_ONTO_L
540.title: Ambidextrous Throwover
540.description:
Entry mode designed for one-handed typing.
Characters from the opposite hand are mirrored to the hand which activates this feature.

542.name: MIRROR_L_ONTO_R
542.title: Ambidextrous Throwover
542.description:
Entry mode designed for one-handed typing.
Characters from the opposite hand are mirrored to the hand which activates this feature.

548.title: Primary Layer
548.description: Can be used as a toggle if you assign a different key to its target layer.

549.name: PRIMARY_KEYMAP_R
549.title: Primary Layer
549.description: Can be used as a toggle if you assign a different key to its target layer.

573.description:
Capitalizes the next chord, or the current chord when used as an
output of a modifier.
573.title: Capitalize

574.title: Join
574.description: Delete the concatenator of the previous chord

545.title: Hyperspace
545.description: Insert the current concatenator set by Hyperspace Capture

547.title: Hyperspace Capture
547.description:
Use in a chord output to capture hyperspace outputs.
You have to put this action both at the start and at the end of what you are capturing.

558.title: Dynamic Library
558.description:
Allows for the activation & creation of dynamic chord libraries.
When included as part of a chord output,
that chord's input becomes the seed for a dynamic chord library,
and that library is activated.
Any new chords created while a dynamic library is active are established one level above its seed.

559.title: Base Library
559.description:
Re-activates your base chord library,
and deactivates any currently active dynamic chord library.

523.title: Press and do not release the next key/action

524.title: Release the next key/action in the sequence

name: Pseudo Actions
description: These actions don't do anything, and can be used for single-key chords

531.title: VIM Mode
531.description:
Enter universal VIM emulation mode.
Equivalent to normal mode in VIM.
Supports a subset of VIM motions in any text box.

532.name: GTM_TOGGLE
532.title: GTM Toggle
532.description:
Text based menu which is accessible anywhere you can type.
It allows you to access various device settings and features
without the need for software and is activated by chording both alt keys together.

534.name: IMPULSE_TOGGLE
534.title: Impulse Toggle
534.description: An 'on-the-fly' custom chord which can be spontaneously created anywhere that you can type via the GTM

538.name: SPUR_TOGGLE
538.title: Spurring Toggle
538.description:
'Chording only' mode which tells your device to output chords on a press rather than a press & release.
It also enables you to jump from one chord to another without releasing everything and can be activated
in GTM or by chording both mirror keys. It can provide significant speed gains with chording,
but also takes away the flexibility of character entry.
Spurring also helps new users learn how to chord by eliminating the need to focus on timing.
Spurring is toggled by chording both of the 'mirror' keys together.

576.title: Active Quickfix
576.description:
Delete the last chord attempt and enter active mode,
where each new attempt replaces the previous one until
a successful chord attempt is hit or escape was pressed.

name: International
description:
Macros for the US-Intl. layout.
Exact mappings may vary depending on the system and specific variation of the layout.

name: Windows Alt Codes
description: Special characters that can be inserted on Windows by holding ALT and typing a code on the Numpad.

796.name: ISO_BACKSLASH
796.title: Keyboard Non-US \ and | (US English)
796.description: On ISO keyboards (L-shaped ENTER key) this extra key is found to the right of the left shift key.

797.name: ISO_PIPE
797.description: On ISO keyboards (L-shaped ENTER key) this extra key is found to the right of the left shift key.

358.title: Keyboard Power
358.description: Only officially supported by Mac and Unix

306.title: ISO Hashpound
306.description:
On ISO keyboards (L-shaped ENTER key) this key is found to the bottom left of the ENTER key.
It is effectively the same as the ANSI Backslash key.
